textPlain: I am a big VCSEL fan. It makes tons of sense to have a laser that you can manufacture at chip scale. I think that VCSEL and SPAD technology is promising in both telecommunications and lidar. History tells us that the cheaper technology gets better faster than the expensive technology gets cheaper.
Sorry, waveguide? For light? I thought that’s what fibre was.

It is. But it’s not the only possible waveguide. The article discusses alternatives for a system that can transmit 5-10k signals in 32U. If you’re using fiber optic cables, that’s 5-10k pairs, in 32U. That doesn’t work at all. It’s light (ha, pun) on details for what the alternative waveguide is, though.Just getting VCELs to 25G per lane would be nice, though, if you can match current prices. Upgrading to 100G-SR4 on the same cable plant would be nice.
